sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish on PayPal Holdings Stock?
feed.summary: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) has underperformed the broader market, with its stock down 38.5% over the past 52 weeks and 4.9% year-to-date, despite a recent 3.9% rise following strong Q3 2025 results and a new partnership with OpenAI. Analysts currently have a "Hold" consensus rating on PYPL, with the mean price target of $72.72 suggesting a 31% premium, though this sentiment is less bullish than three months ago. The company's adjusted EPS is expected to grow by 15.3% year-over-year for the current fiscal year.

feed.title: MSCI Reports Financial Results for Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, with double-digit growth in operating revenues and adjusted EPS. The company saw significant increases across its Index, Analytics, Sustainability and Climate, and Private Assets segments, driven by recurring subscriptions and asset-based fees. MSCI anticipates continued growth in 2026, supported by strategic initiatives and a focus on AI integration.

feed.title: WeRide launches GENESIS simulation platform to accelerate global autonomous driving development
feed.summary: Autonomous driving company WeRide has launched GENESIS, a new simulation platform that uses a blend of physical and generative AI to accelerate the development and commercialization of autonomous driving systems. GENESIS is designed to replicate complex urban environments and rare scenarios, significantly reducing the need for extensive on-road testing. The platform features a closed-loop architecture with four core AI modules for scenario generation, agent behavior modeling, performance evaluation, and automated diagnosis, aiming to refine autonomous driving algorithms continuously.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Johnson & Johnson, sees upside on robust drug pipeline
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Johnson & Johnson to an overweight rating, increasing its price target to $262 due to a robust drug pipeline and strong earnings potential. Analyst Terence Flynn highlights the company's favorable revenue and EPS growth projections, anticipating significant contributions from new products that are expected to offset patent expirations. This upgrade comes as Johnson & Johnson shares have performed well, reflecting positive analyst sentiment.

feed.title: Elevance Health: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Elevance Health Inc. reported Q4 earnings of $547 million, or $2.47 per share, with adjusted earnings of $3.33 per share, surpassing analyst expectations. The health insurer posted revenue of $49.75 billion, though its adjusted revenue of $49.31 billion missed Street forecasts. For the full year, the company had a profit of $5.66 billion on revenue of $197.58 billion and expects full-year earnings of $25.50 per share.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines orders 31 widebody Airbus jets to increase its international capacity (DAL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has placed a firm order for 31 widebody Airbus jets, including 16 A330-900s and 15 A350-900s, to enhance its international capacity. The agreement with Airbus also allows for an option to purchase up to 20 additional widebody aircraft. This move aims to expand Delta's global reach and strengthen its fleet for long-haul routes.

feed.title: Lennox Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results; Provides Full Year 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Lennox (NYSE: LII) announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, successfully navigating industry challenges to achieve annual margins over 20% for the first time in its history. The company reported $1.2 billion in revenue for Q4 2025 (down 11%) and $5.2 billion for the full year (down 3%), along with adjusted diluted EPS of $4.45 for Q4 (down 22%) and $23.16 for the full year (up 2%). Lennox also provided full-year 2026 guidance, expecting revenue to increase by approximately 6% to 7% and adjusted earnings per share between $23.50 and $25.00.

feed.title: Will Oppenheimer's Downgrade and Rising Short Interest Change Emerson Electric's (EMR) Narrative?
feed.summary: Oppenheimer recently downgraded Emerson Electric (EMR) from Outperform to Perform, citing the company's valuation already reflecting strong growth expectations. Despite EMR's rising share price and potential undervaluation, the downgrade, coupled with increased short interest, emphasizes valuation and debt concerns. Investors are encouraged to consider multiple perspectives and perform their own research, particularly ahead of the upcoming earnings release.

feed.title: ADM Settles SEC Case and DOJ Closes Probe
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) has settled with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding intersegment sales reporting, agreeing to pay a $40 million civil penalty without admitting wrongdoing. The Department of Justice also closed its related investigation without further action. ADM stated that the errors were limited to segment-level reporting, had no impact on consolidated financial statements, and has since restated relevant filings and improved internal controls.

feed.title: Kimco Realty announces retirement of chief accounting officer, appoints successor
feed.summary: Kimco Realty Corporation announced the retirement of Paul Westbrook, Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, effective March 31, 2026. Kathleen Thayer, currently Senior Vice President and Treasurer, Corporate Accounting, will succeed him as Executive Vice President, Treasurer, and Chief Accounting Officer on April 1, 2026. This leadership change occurs amidst recent financial activities for Kimco, including a $750 million stock offering and an upgrade in its senior unsecured ratings to A3 by Moody’s Ratings.
